evidence_rows.reading: The first v4 lane should be judged using the strongest q2/A symbol rather than widening to another lane.
A clean opening-led reading needs aligned permission and entry family, with anchors blocked mainly by hierarchy admission.
If the checked late window does not preserve the specialist position while anchors churn out, the lane should not be called persistence-led.

interpretation: The first v4 lane should only upgrade the carry reading if it contributes more than another clean opening case.
The current 601919 evidence shows a specialist-only opening edge with aligned permission and entry family, but no clean persistence edge in the checked late window.
So the correct current posture is to treat the first v4 lane as opening-led and keep the second lane closed until a later lane changes the carry-row-diversity reading.
